title: Neonauclea dahlii
taxonomy: Neonauclea dahlii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Neonauclea, which is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of Asia. Neonauclea dahlii is further classified as a member of the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant is also classified as a dicotyledon, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage. This classification helps to organize and understand the relationships between different plant species and their evolutionary history.

intro: Neonauclea dahlii, also known as Dahl's Neonauclea, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia.
